Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants a DuckDB SQL query that filters out specific cases from an event_log table The cases to be excluded are those that contain a specific sequence of three activities in direct succession Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order AND where the time difference between Create Order and Confirm Order is greater than 5 days The final output should be all events from cases that do not meet these criteria

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Input Table Structure Table event_log Columns case_id activity timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Requirements Identify cases with the exact sequence Approve Purchase Requisition Create Order Confirm Order direct succession no other events in between Check time difference Exclude these cases from the final result Return all events from the remaining cases Ensure proper ordering grouping typically ordered by case_id timestamp

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: DuckDB Specifics DuckDB supports window functions LAG LEAD ROW_NUMBER DuckDB supports date time arithmetic INTERVAL AGE or direct subtraction DuckDB supports QUALIFY clause WITH EXCEPT NOT IN NOT EXISTS Need to handle direct succession carefully This means for a given case when events are ordered by timestamp the activity immediately following Approve Purchase Requisition must be Create Order and the one immediately following that must be Confirm Order

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Step 1 Identify the sequence and time condition I need to find case_ids that match the criteria I can use window functions to look at the next two activities for each event Alternatively I can self join or use LEAD activity 1 and LEAD activity 2 Lets use LEAD

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now filter for the sequence activity equals Approve Purchase Requisition next_activity equals Create Order next_next_activity equals Confirm Order And time condition next_next_timestamp minus next_timestamp greater than INTERVAL 5 days

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Step 2 Exclude these cases and return all other events SELECT FROM event_log WHERE case_id NOT IN SELECT case_id FROM MatchedCases ORDER BY case_id timestamp

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Step 3 Refine and Check Edge Cases What if there are ties in timestamps The prompt says direct succession which usually implies ordering by timestamp If timestamps are identical ordering might be ambiguous I will assume standard ORDER BY timestamp is sufficient Need to verify the time condition This matches next_next_ts minus next_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 days If the sequence appears multiple times in a case If it appears at least once with the time condition the case should be excluded DISTINCT case_id handles this

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification during thought Is LEAD safe for direct succession Yes because LEAD activity 1 gets the very next row in the partition order If there is any event in between it would be offset 1 not 2 So LEAD correctly enforces direct succession

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: What about ties in timestamp In process mining events with identical timestamps might have arbitrary order To be robust I could add a secondary sort key if available but the prompt only gives timestamp I will stick to ORDER BY timestamp as it is standard

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: NOT IN can be problematic if case_id is NULL but case_id is an identifier likely not null I will use NOT EXISTS or LEFT JOIN IS NULL for safety or just NOT IN since it is standard NOT IN is fine here

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Alternatively DATEDIFF day create_order_ts confirm_order_ts greater than 5 is more explicit Actually DATEDIFF day counts day boundaries which might differ from exact 24 times 5 hours The prompt says more than 5 days which usually means greater than 120 hours I will use confirm_order_ts minus create_order_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 days as it is precise

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets refine the CTE structure for maximum DuckDB compatibility and performance

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check DuckDB syntax WINDOW w AS is valid LEAD is valid INTERVAL 5 days is valid NOT IN is valid ORDER BY is valid LEAD might return NULL if there are not enough following rows The WHERE clause handles this naturally

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Could also use MATCH_RECOGNIZE in DuckDB for pattern matching but window functions are simpler and widely supported I will stick to window functions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check of the prompt direct succession with no other event from the same case in between them LEAD guarantees this time elapsed more than 5 days confirm_order_ts minus create_order_ts greater than INTERVAL 5 days exclude them from your final result WHERE case_id NOT IN ensure proper grouping ordering and filtering ORDER BY case_id timestamp All constraints met
